extension_name: Devinization Pack 4 — Execution Safety, Tools & Recovery

included_families.family_name: Agent Tool Safety, Sandboxing & Permission Firewalls

purpose: Define the execution-safety layer that prevents the Station Chief runtime and future agent workforce from mutating the wrong files, wasting credits, making unsafe external actions, hiding failures, claiming fake completion, leaking secrets, skipping validators, or turning a small repair into a new disaster.
core_operating_philosophy: The Agent Command Center remains a massive command civilization with selective live deployment. The whole 175-family agency remains available for awareness, routing, governance, memory, and context. This pack does not shrink the art piece. This pack defines how the system executes safely, verifies honestly, recovers cleanly, and proves completion before saying done.
activation_rule: Use this pack when the user says or implies: run the tool, use GitHub, check the repo, verify the commit, external action, permission, sandbox, safe execution, tool call, API call, file operation, test this, validate this, smoke test, integration test, failed run, error recovery, rollback, repair, scope drift, wrong files changed, no output, credit burn, done done, final lock, definition of done, prove it passed, changed files, validator results.

execution_safety_layers.name: Permission Boundary
execution_safety_layers.purpose: Decide whether the system may answer, inspect, edit, run, commit, push, or perform an external action.

execution_safety_layers.name: Scope Boundary
execution_safety_layers.purpose: Restrict execution to allowed files, allowed commands, allowed tools, and declared outputs.

execution_safety_layers.name: Tool Safety Boundary
execution_safety_layers.purpose: Prevent unsafe, irrelevant, expensive, or unauthorized tool calls.

execution_safety_layers.name: Execution Evidence Boundary
execution_safety_layers.purpose: Require changed-file lists, validator output, run logs, commit hashes, and proof artifacts.

execution_safety_layers.name: Recovery Boundary
execution_safety_layers.purpose: Detect failures, isolate overreach, repair only the broken area, and validate after repair.

execution_safety_layers.name: Completion Boundary
execution_safety_layers.purpose: Block fake completion unless required artifacts, validators, reports, and final proof are present.

global_rules: 1. Preserve the locked 175-family baseline unless the user explicitly authorizes baseline mutation.
2. Use overlays for Devinization unless explicitly instructed otherwise.
4. Repository tasks must include changed-file scope.
5. Repository tasks must include validator results or a clear reason validators were not applicable.
6. Execution tasks must produce an output artifact or a blocked-reason artifact.
7. External actions require explicit permission unless already authorized by task scope.
8. Secret, credential, token, and private-data handling must stay inside safe boundaries.
9. Do not run expensive live execution when deterministic demo mode can prove the workflow first.
11. If wrong files are modified, stop and report before continuing.
12. Repair work must be minimal and targeted.
13. Do not use apology as a substitute for repair.
14. Error reports must include cause, affected files, repair plan, and validation plan.
15. Completion requires objective, outputs, scope check, validator check, and final report.
16. Done means done-done, not probably fine.
17. Preserve the art piece: safety controls protect the full agency without shrinking the command civilization.

crews.mission: Maintain a registry of available tools, what each tool can do, what it cannot do, permission requirements, risk level, and expected output evidence.
Define safe adapter boundaries for file operations, GitHub operations, browser/search operations, local scripts, APIs, and future MCP-style connectors.
Handle GitHub-specific verification workflows including commit checks, diffs, file scope, status, rollback evidence, and commit-hash reporting.
handoff_targets: DV.P4.041 Final Report / Commit Proof Crew

crews.mission: Control file reads, writes, deletes, generated artifacts, untracked files, scope checks, and file-system cleanup rules.
handoff_targets: DV.P4.032 Allowed / Forbidden File Guard Crew
DV.P4.034 Scope Drift Runtime Guard Crew

crews.mission: Govern API calls, request/response logging, rate limits, credential boundaries, fallback behavior, and no-key demo-mode alternatives.
Detect failed, partial, unavailable, misleading, or stale tool results and route them into recovery instead of pretending success.
Classify errors as scope drift, wrong file mutation, validator failure, missing output, tool failure, prompt failure, runtime failure, or user-intent mismatch.
Detect when the system changed more than requested, edited forbidden files, expanded scope, normalized content, or “improved” beyond instruction.
handoff_targets: DV.P4.034 Scope Drift Runtime Guard Crew

crews.mission: Create safe rollback, revert, or cleanup plans that isolate the bad change without damaging valid work.
Generate precise repair prompts that fix only the failed portion, preserve valid work, and enforce allowed-file scope.
handoff_targets: DV.P4.034 Scope Drift Runtime Guard Crew
DV.P4.041 Final Report / Commit Proof Crew

crews.mission: Report failures and repairs plainly without fake certainty, over-apologizing, burying the problem, or using apology as a substitute for correction.
handoff_targets: DV.P4.041 Final Report / Commit Proof Crew
DV.P4.042 No Output Failure & Blocked Reason Crew

crews.mission: Run or require validation after repair, including validators, changed-file checks, report checks, and confirmation that the original error is fixed.
handoff_targets: DV.P4.041 Final Report / Commit Proof Crew

crews.mission: Define integration test plans that verify connected pieces work together across prompts, runtime, files, validators, reports, and tool operations.
Create or run quick smoke tests proving the system can complete the smallest valid workflow from input to output evidence.
Validate workflows with deterministic demo data before using live APIs, paid model calls, credentials, or external actions.
Check that new changes do not break prior packs, validators, baseline locks, reports, or already-working runtime behavior.
handoff_targets: DV.P4.041 Final Report / Commit Proof Crew

crews.mission: Define and verify the correct chain of validators for each task, including pack validators, baseline validators, runtime validators, and report validators.
handoff_targets: DV.P4.041 Final Report / Commit Proof Crew

crews.mission: Bundle test results, validator outputs, run logs, failure notes, changed files, and proof artifacts into a reviewable evidence packet.
handoff_targets: DV.P4.041 Final Report / Commit Proof Crew
DV.P4.045 Source Preservation Crew if available

crews.mission: Track each run’s objective, selected crews, tools used, commands run, outputs created, validators run, errors, and final state.
handoff_targets: DV.P4.022 Model / Tool Call Trace Crew

crews.mission: Maintain readable execution logs that show what happened, in what order, what succeeded, what failed, and what remains.
handoff_targets: DV.P4.041 Final Report / Commit Proof Crew

crews.mission: Track and limit expensive model calls, tool calls, retries, long-running sessions, and no-output loops so runtime execution stays useful.
Record which model/tool calls happened, why they were needed, what they returned, and whether the result was used.
Identify runtime slowdowns, repeated failures, unnecessary calls, redundant validation, oversized context, and inefficient execution loops.
Summarize active run health, validator health, error health, budget health, output health, and completion readiness.
handoff_targets: DV.P4.041 Final Report / Commit Proof Crew
DV.P4.042 No Output Failure & Blocked Reason Crew

crews.mission: Decide whether the system is allowed to perform external actions such as API calls, pushes, deletes, sends, deployments, or irreversible changes.
handoff_targets: DV.P4.028 Network / Write Action Gate Crew

crews.mission: Require human confirmation for risky, irreversible, external, sensitive, or scope-expanding actions.
handoff_targets: DV.P4.035 Data / Secret Leak Prevention Crew

crews.mission: Protect API keys, tokens, secrets, credentials, private data, environment files, and sensitive values from exposure or unsafe use.
handoff_targets: DV.P4.035 Data / Secret Leak Prevention Crew

crews.mission: Gate network access, writes, commits, pushes, file deletes, deployments, and external mutations based on task scope and permission level.
handoff_targets: DV.P4.032 Allowed / Forbidden File Guard Crew

crews.mission: Audit any external change after it happens, including what changed, where, why, with what proof, and how to revert.
handoff_targets: DV.P4.041 Final Report / Commit Proof Crew

crews.mission: Provide deterministic demo, local-only, no-key, no-credential, or offline fallback paths when live credentials are missing or expensive.

crews.mission: Monitor runtime execution for actions, files, goals, or outputs that drift beyond the approved operation brief.

crews.family_anchor: 144. Agent Tool Safety, Sandboxing & Permission Firewalls
crews.mission: Prevent secrets, credentials, private data, hidden tokens, sensitive paths, or unsafe content from being exposed in outputs, logs, commits, or reports.
handoff_targets: DV.P4.041 Final Report / Commit Proof Crew

crews.family_anchor: 144. Agent Tool Safety, Sandboxing & Permission Firewalls
crews.mission: Validate that sandbox rules, file guards, command blockers, external gates, and secret boundaries are applied before and after execution.
Define the completion standard for each task, including objective satisfied, artifacts created, validators passed, scope verified, and report delivered.
handoff_targets: DV.P4.041 Final Report / Commit Proof Crew

crews.mission: Require every execution task to produce a durable artifact, changed file, diagnostic report, validator result, or blocked-reason artifact.
handoff_targets: DV.P4.042 No Output Failure & Blocked Reason Crew
DV.P4.041 Final Report / Commit Proof Crew

crews.mission: Block completion claims until required validators pass or a valid blocked-reason explanation is produced.
handoff_targets: DV.P4.041 Final Report / Commit Proof Crew

crews.mission: Require repository tasks to provide changed-file lists, staged-file lists, commit diffs, and confirmation that no forbidden files changed.
handoff_targets: DV.P4.032 Allowed / Forbidden File Guard Crew
DV.P4.041 Final Report / Commit Proof Crew

crews.mission: Assemble final reports with files created/modified, validators run, pass/fail status, commit hash, scope confirmation, and next step.

crews.crew_name: No Output Failure & Blocked Reason Crew
crews.mission: Treat no-output runs as failures unless a clear blocked-reason artifact explains what stopped, what was attempted, and what exact next step is needed.
